JK Connections As mentioned earlier, our best performing model uses a JK-Connections in the scoring function, as given by, score(u,rt,v) = WT L⨁ i=1 [hi G(u,v,rt) ⊕hi u ⊕hi v ⊕ert]. (8) This is inspired by (Xu et al., 2018) which lets the model adapt the effective neighborhood size for each node as needed. Empirically, this made our model’s performance more robust to other number of GNN layers. B. Other Model Variants As mentioned in Section 3.1, the formulation of our GNN scoring model allows for ﬂexibility to plug in different AGGREGATE and COMBINE functions. We experi- mented with pooling AGGRAGATE function Hamilton et al. (2017b) and two other COMBINE function (similar to CON- CAT operation from Hamilton et al. (2017b) and using a GRU as in Li et al. (2016)). None of these variants gave signiﬁcant improvements in the performance. C. Hyperparameter Settings The model was implemented in PyTorch. Experiments were run for 50 epochs on a GTX 1080 Ti with 12 GB RAM. The Adam optimizer was used with a learning rate of 0.01, L2 penalty of 5e-4, and default values for other parameters. The margin in the loss was set to 10. Gradient were clipped at a norm of 1000. The model was evaluated on the valida- tion and saved every three epochs with the best performing checkpoint used for testing. D. Transductive Results The transductive results, as mentioned in the discussion on inductive results (Section 4.1), were obtained using the the same methodology of the aforementioned evaluations. In particular, GraIL was trained on the train-graph and tested n the same. We randomly selected 10% of the links in train- graph as test links. Tables 8 and 9 showcase the transductive results. The AUC-PR and Hits@10 in the transductive set- ting are signiﬁcantly better than in the inductive setting, establishing the difﬁculty of the inductive task. GraIL per- forms signiﬁcantly better than RuleN in most cases and is competitive in others. E. Inductive Graph Generation The inductive train and test graphs examined in this paper do not have overlapping entities. To generate the train graph we sampled several entities uniformly to serve as roots then took the union of the k-hop neighborhoods surrounding the roots. We capped the number of new neighbors at each hop to prevent exponential growth. We remove the samples training graph from the whole graph and sample the test graph using the same procedure. The parameters of the above process are adjusted to obtain a series of graphs of increasing size. The statistics of different datasets collected in this manner are summarized in Table 13. Overall, we generate four versions of inductive datasets from each knowledge graph with increasing sizes. G. Proof of Theorem 1 We restate the main Theorem for completeness. Theorem 2. We also have the ﬂexibility of learning appropriate embeddings of the relations in 1- dimensional space. This is possible to an arbitrary degree of precision given that MLP with non-linear activation, as is our case, is a universal function approximator (Hornik, 1991). Lemma 2. llm_extracted_info.main_contributions: Addresses inductive relation prediction in knowledge graphs, where entities at test time are unseen during training. Proposes GraIL, a GNN-based subgraph reasoning framework that learns entity-independent relational semantics by operating on local enclosing subgraphs rather than entity embeddings. Provides theoretical proof that GraIL can encode and count path-based first-order logical rules. Introduces new fully-inductive benchmarks (disjoint-entity splits) for WN18RR, FB15k-237, and NELL-995. Demonstrates state-of-the-art inductive performance over rule-induction baselines and complementary gains via ensembling with KGE models in the transductive setting.
llm_extracted_info.methodology: GraIL scores a candidate triple (u, rt, v) via: (1) Enclosing subgraph extraction: build the subgraph induced by nodes on paths between u and v using intersection of k-hop neighborhoods and pruning; edge directions ignored during extraction but used in message passing. (2) Node labeling: Double Radius Vertex Labeling (DRNL) assigns each node a pair of distances (d(i,u), d(i,v)) with one-hot features; targets uniquely labeled (0,1) and (1,0). (3) GNN scoring: multi-relational message passing with relation-specific weights (basis decomposition), edge dropout, and edge attention α that depends on source/target node states, edge type r, and target relation rt (sigmoid gate). Node updates are R-GCN-like (ReLU(W_self h_t + aggregated messages)). After L layers, average-pool node embeddings to a subgraph representation and concatenate with target node embeddings and a learned embedding of rt for final linear scoring. Uses JK connections to concatenate intermediate layer representations. Trained with margin-ranking loss and negative sampling (replace head or tail). Theoretically encodes path rules; corollary shows counting satisfied rules.
llm_extracted_info.experimental_setup: Datasets/benchmarks: Standard WN18RR, FB15k-237, NELL-995 for transductive; new fully-inductive benchmarks created by sampling disjoint train/test subgraphs with identical relation sets (four versions v1–v4 of increasing size per dataset). NELL-995 transductive split created as 70/15/15 ensuring all entities/relations appear in train. Baselines: Inductive—NeuralLP, DRUM, RuleN (rules up to length 4). Transductive—TransE, DistMult, ComplEx, RotatE (Sun et al. 2019 implementation; self-adversarial negative sampling disabled). Model/config: For inductive experiments, 3-hop enclosing subgraphs and 3-layer GNN; latent dim 32, basis dim 4, edge dropout 0.5. Transductive GraIL uses 2-hop (WN18RR, NELL-995) and 1-hop (FB15k-237). Training: Adam (lr 0.01), L2 5e-4, margin 10, 50 epochs, gradient clipping; negative sampling by random head/tail replacement. Evaluation: AUC-PR (classification) and Hits@10 (ranking); for ranking, each positive is ranked among 50 sampled negatives. Ensembling: late fusion—concatenate method scores and train a linear classifier on validation; also early fusion (GraIL++) by concatenating DRNL features with pre-trained TransE embeddings. Results: GraIL outperforms NeuralLP/DRUM/RuleN on inductive AUC-PR and Hits@10 (avg relative gains ≈5.25% AUC-PR, 6.75% Hits@10 vs strongest baseline). In transductive, ensembling GraIL with KGEs yields consistent gains on WN18RR and NELL-995 (average gains ≈1.5% and 0.62%), little/no gain on FB15k-237. Early fusion improves GraIL AUC-PR (e.g., WN18RR 90.91→96.20). Ablations show enclosing subgraph extraction, DRNL labeling, and attention are critical.
llm_extracted_info.limitations: Assumes identical relation vocabulary (schema) between training and inductive test graphs; cannot handle unseen relation types. Relies solely on structural information (no node/edge attributes or text), which may limit performance where attributes are important. Enclosing subgraph extraction ignores edge direction (direction used only during message passing), which may discard potentially useful constraints. Inference is more expensive than embedding-only methods due to per-query subgraph extraction and shortest-path computations; complexity scales with subgraph size. Theoretical expressivity covers path-based Horn clauses; more complex non-path logical structures are not guaranteed. Performance without ensembling is not state-of-the-art in transductive settings and ensemble gains are dataset-dependent (limited on FB15k-237). Requires choosing hop parameter k; overly large k can cause overfitting and computational overhead.
llm_extracted_info.future_research_directions:
- Extract interpretable logical rules and structural patterns from trained GraIL models.
- Improve scalability: faster/lighter subgraph extraction, sampling, or caching; approximate labeling.
- Extend expressivity beyond simple path rules to handle branched rules, cycles, and more general first-order logic.
- Integrate node/edge attributes and textual signals; joint training with KGE models (early fusion, co-training).
- Meta-learning and few-shot adaptation for new relations and rapid generalization.
- Robustness analysis under distribution shifts in relation frequencies and graph structure; domain adaptation across graphs.
- Adaptive/learned subgraph selection and hop size; incorporate edge direction during extraction.
- Optimize inference (e.g., batching, index structures) and explore real-time/streaming settings for dynamic KGs.
llm_extracted_info.experimental_code:

import os

import struct

import random
import pickle as pkl
import pdb
from tqdm import tqdm
import lmdb
import multiprocessing as mp
import numpy as np
import scipy.io as sio
import scipy.sparse as ssp
import sys
import torch
from scipy.special import softmax
from utils.dgl_utils import _bfs_relational
from utils.graph_utils import incidence_matrix, remove_nodes, ssp_to_torch, serialize, deserialize, get_edge_count, diameter, radius
import networkx as nx


def sample_neg(adj_list, edges, num_neg_samples_per_link=1, max_size=1000000, constrained_neg_prob=0):
    pos_edges = edges
    neg_edges = []

    # if max_size is set, randomly sample train links
    if max_size < len(pos_edges):
        perm = np.random.permutation(len(pos_edges))[:max_size]
        pos_edges = pos_edges[perm]

    # sample negative links for train/test
    n, r = adj_list[0].shape[0], len(adj_list)

    # distribution of edges across reelations
    theta = 0.001
    edge_count = get_edge_count(adj_list)
    rel_dist = np.zeros(edge_count.shape)
    idx = np.nonzero(edge_count)
    rel_dist[idx] = softmax(theta * edge_count[idx])

    # possible head and tails for each relation
    valid_heads = [adj.tocoo().row.tolist() for adj in adj_list]
    valid_tails = [adj.tocoo().col.tolist() for adj in adj_list]

    pbar = tqdm(total=len(pos_edges))
    while len(neg_edges) < num_neg_samples_per_link * len(pos_edges):
        neg_head, neg_tail, rel = pos_edges[pbar.n % len(pos_edges)][0], pos_edges[pbar.n % len(pos_edges)][1], pos_edges[pbar.n % len(pos_edges)][2]
        if np.random.uniform() < constrained_neg_prob:
            if np.random.uniform() < 0.5:
                neg_head = np.random.choice(valid_heads[rel])
            else:
                neg_tail = np.random.choice(valid_tails[rel])
        else:
            if np.random.uniform() < 0.5:
                neg_head = np.random.choice(n)
            else:
                neg_tail = np.random.choice(n)

        if neg_head != neg_tail and adj_list[rel][neg_head, neg_tail] == 0:
            neg_edges.append([neg_head, neg_tail, rel])
            pbar.update(1)

    pbar.close()

    neg_edges = np.array(neg_edges)
    return pos_edges, neg_edges


def links2subgraphs(A, graphs, params, max_label_value=None):
    '''
    extract enclosing subgraphs, write map mode + named dbs
    '''
    max_n_label = {'value': np.array([0, 0])}
    subgraph_sizes = []
    enc_ratios = []
    num_pruned_nodes = []

    BYTES_PER_DATUM = get_average_subgraph_size(100, list(graphs.values())[0]['pos'], A, params) * 1.5
    links_length = 0
    for split_name, split in graphs.items():
        links_length += (len(split['pos']) + len(split['neg'])) * 2
    map_size = links_length * BYTES_PER_DATUM

    env = lmdb.open(params.db_path, map_size=map_size, max_dbs=6)

    def extraction_helper(A, links, g_labels, split_env):

        with env.begin(write=True, db=split_env) as txn:
            txn.put('num_graphs'.encode(), (len(links)).to_bytes(int.bit_length(len(links)), byteorder='little'))

        with mp.Pool(processes=None, initializer=intialize_worker, initargs=(A, params, max_label_value)) as p:
            args_ = zip(range(len(links)), links, g_labels)
            for (str_id, datum) in tqdm(p.imap(extract_save_subgraph, args_), total=len(links)):
                max_n_label['value'] = np.maximum(np.max(datum['n_labels'], axis=0), max_n_label['value'])
                subgraph_sizes.append(datum['subgraph_size'])
                enc_ratios.append(datum['enc_ratio'])
                num_pruned_nodes.append(datum['num_pruned_nodes'])

                with env.begin(write=True, db=split_env) as txn:
                    txn.put(str_id, serialize(datum))

    for split_name, split in graphs.items():
        logging.info(f"Extracting enclosing subgraphs for positive links in {split_name} set")
        labels = np.ones(len(split['pos']))
        db_name_pos = split_name + '_pos'
        split_env = env.open_db(db_name_pos.encode())
        extraction_helper(A, split['pos'], labels, split_env)

        logging.info(f"Extracting enclosing subgraphs for negative links in {split_name} set")
        labels = np.zeros(len(split['neg']))
        db_name_neg = split_name + '_neg'
        split_env = env.open_db(db_name_neg.encode())
        extraction_helper(A, split['neg'], labels, split_env)

    max_n_label['value'] = max_label_value if max_label_value is not None else max_n_label['value']

    with env.begin(write=True) as txn:
        bit_len_label_sub = int.bit_length(int(max_n_label['value'][0]))
        bit_len_label_obj = int.bit_length(int(max_n_label['value'][1]))
        txn.put('max_n_label_sub'.encode(), (int(max_n_label['value'][0])).to_bytes(bit_len_label_sub, byteorder='little'))
        txn.put('max_n_label_obj'.encode(), (int(max_n_label['value'][1])).to_bytes(bit_len_label_obj, byteorder='little'))

        txn.put('avg_subgraph_size'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.mean(subgraph_sizes))))
        txn.put('min_subgraph_size'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.min(subgraph_sizes))))
        txn.put('max_subgraph_size'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.max(subgraph_sizes))))
        txn.put('std_subgraph_size'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.std(subgraph_sizes))))

        txn.put('avg_enc_ratio'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.mean(enc_ratios))))
        txn.put('min_enc_ratio'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.min(enc_ratios))))
        txn.put('max_enc_ratio'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.max(enc_ratios))))
        txn.put('std_enc_ratio'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.std(enc_ratios))))

        txn.put('avg_num_pruned_nodes'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.mean(num_pruned_nodes))))
        txn.put('min_num_pruned_nodes'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.min(num_pruned_nodes))))
        txn.put('max_num_pruned_nodes'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.max(num_pruned_nodes))))
        txn.put('std_num_pruned_nodes'.encode(), struct.pack('f', float(np.std(num_pruned_nodes))))


def get_average_subgraph_size(sample_size, links, A, params):
    total_size = 0
    for (n1, n2, r_label) in links[np.random.choice(len(links), sample_size)]:
        nodes, n_labels, subgraph_size, enc_ratio, num_pruned_nodes = subgraph_extraction_labeling((n1, n2), r_label, A, params.hop, params.enclosing_sub_graph, params.max_nodes_per_hop)
        datum = {'nodes': nodes, 'r_label': r_label, 'g_label': 0, 'n_labels': n_labels, 'subgraph_size': subgraph_size, 'enc_ratio': enc_ratio, 'num_pruned_nodes': num_pruned_nodes}
        total_size += len(serialize(datum))
    return total_size / sample_size


def intialize_worker(A, params, max_label_value):
    global A_, params_, max_label_value_
    A_, params_, max_label_value_ = A, params, max_label_value


def extract_save_subgraph(args_):
    idx, (n1, n2, r_label), g_label = args_
    nodes, n_labels, subgraph_size, enc_ratio, num_pruned_nodes = subgraph_extraction_labeling((n1, n2), r_label, A_, params_.hop, params_.enclosing_sub_graph, params_.max_nodes_per_hop)

    # max_label_value_ is to set the maximum possible value of node label while doing double-radius labelling.
    if max_label_value_ is not None:
        n_labels = np.array([np.minimum(label, max_label_value_).tolist() for label in n_labels])

    datum = {'nodes': nodes, 'r_label': r_label, 'g_label': g_label, 'n_labels': n_labels, 'subgraph_size': subgraph_size, 'enc_ratio': enc_ratio, 'num_pruned_nodes': num_pruned_nodes}
    str_id = '{:08}'.format(idx).encode('ascii')

    return (str_id, datum)


def get_neighbor_nodes(roots, adj, h=1, max_nodes_per_hop=None):
    bfs_generator = _bfs_relational(adj, roots, max_nodes_per_hop)
    lvls = list()
    for _ in range(h):
        try:
            lvls.append(next(bfs_generator))
        except StopIteration:
            pass
    return set().union(*lvls)


def subgraph_extraction_labeling(ind, rel, A_list, h=1, enclosing_sub_graph=False, max_nodes_per_hop=None, max_node_label_value=None):
    # extract the h-hop enclosing subgraphs around link 'ind'
    A_incidence = incidence_matrix(A_list)
    A_incidence += A_incidence.T

    root1_nei = get_neighbor_nodes(set([ind[0]]), A_incidence, h, max_nodes_per_hop)
    root2_nei = get_neighbor_nodes(set([ind[1]]), A_incidence, h, max_nodes_per_hop)

    subgraph_nei_nodes_int = root1_nei.intersection(root2_nei)
    subgraph_nei_nodes_un = root1_nei.union(root2_nei)

    # Extract subgraph | Roots being in the front is essential for labelling and the model to work properly.
    if enclosing_sub_graph:
        subgraph_nodes = list(ind) + list(subgraph_nei_nodes_int)
    else:
        subgraph_nodes = list(ind) + list(subgraph_nei_nodes_un)

    subgraph = [adj[subgraph_nodes, :][:, subgraph_nodes] for adj in A_list]

    labels, enclosing_subgraph_nodes = node_label(incidence_matrix(subgraph), max_distance=h)

    pruned_subgraph_nodes = np.array(subgraph_nodes)[enclosing_subgraph_nodes].tolist()
    pruned_labels = labels[enclosing_subgraph_nodes]

    if max_node_label_value is not None:
        pruned_labels = np.array([np.minimum(label, max_node_label_value).tolist() for label in pruned_labels])

    subgraph_size = len(pruned_subgraph_nodes)
    enc_ratio = len(subgraph_nei_nodes_int) / (len(subgraph_nei_nodes_un) + 1e-3)
    num_pruned_nodes = len(subgraph_nodes) - len(pruned_subgraph_nodes)

    return pruned_subgraph_nodes, pruned_labels, subgraph_size, enc_ratio, num_pruned_nodes


def node_label(subgraph, max_distance=1):
    # implementation of the node labeling scheme described in the paper
    roots = [0, 1]
    sgs_single_root = [remove_nodes(subgraph, [root]) for root in roots]
    dist_to_roots = [np.clip(ssp.csgraph.dijkstra(sg, indices=[0], directed=False, unweighted=True, limit=1e6)[:, 1:], 0, 1e7) for r, sg in enumerate(sgs_single_root)]
    dist_to_roots = np.array(list(zip(dist_to_roots[0][0], dist_to_roots[1][0])), dtype=int)

    target_node_labels = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
    labels = np.concatenate((target_node_labels, dist_to_roots)) if dist_to_roots.size else target_node_labels

    enclosing_subgraph_nodes = np.where(np.max(labels, axis=1) <= max_distance)[0]
    return labels, enclosing_subgraph_nodes

from torch.utils.data import Dataset
import timeit
import os
import logging
import lmdb
import numpy as np
import json
import pickle
import dgl
from utils.graph_utils import ssp_multigraph_to_dgl, incidence_matrix
from utils.data_utils import process_files, save_to_file, plot_rel_dist
from .graph_sampler import *
import pdb


def generate_subgraph_datasets(params, splits=['train', 'valid'], saved_relation2id=None, max_label_value=None):

    testing = 'test' in splits
    adj_list, triplets, entity2id, relation2id, id2entity, id2relation = process_files(params.file_paths, saved_relation2id)

    data_path = os.path.join(params.main_dir, f'data/{params.dataset}/relation2id.json')
    if not os.path.isdir(data_path) and not testing:
        with open(data_path, 'w') as f:
            json.dump(relation2id, f)

    graphs = {}

    for split_name in splits:
        graphs[split_name] = {'triplets': triplets[split_name], 'max_size': params.max_links}

    # Sample train and valid/test links
    for split_name, split in graphs.items():
        logging.info(f"Sampling negative links for {split_name}")
        split['pos'], split['neg'] = sample_neg(adj_list, split['triplets'], params.num_neg_samples_per_link, max_size=split['max_size'], constrained_neg_prob=params.constrained_neg_prob)

    if testing:
        directory = os.path.join(params.main_dir, 'data/{}/'.format(params.dataset))
        save_to_file(directory, f'neg_{params.test_file}_{params.constrained_neg_prob}.txt', graphs['test']['neg'], id2entity, id2relation)

    links2subgraphs(adj_list, graphs, params, max_label_value)


def get_kge_embeddings(dataset, kge_model):

    path = './experiments/kge_baselines/{}_{}'.format(kge_model, dataset)
    node_features = np.load(os.path.join(path, 'entity_embedding.npy'))
    with open(os.path.join(path, 'id2entity.json')) as json_file:
        kge_id2entity = json.load(json_file)
        kge_entity2id = {v: int(k) for k, v in kge_id2entity.items()}

    return node_features, kge_entity2id


class SubgraphDataset(Dataset):
    """Extracted, labeled, subgraph dataset -- DGL Only"""

    def __init__(self, db_path, db_name_pos, db_name_neg, raw_data_paths, included_relations=None, add_traspose_rels=False, num_neg_samples_per_link=1, use_kge_embeddings=False, dataset='', kge_model='', file_name=''):

        self.main_env = lmdb.open(db_path, readonly=True, max_dbs=3, lock=False)
        self.db_pos = self.main_env.open_db(db_name_pos.encode())
        self.db_neg = self.main_env.open_db(db_name_neg.encode())
        self.node_features, self.kge_entity2id = get_kge_embeddings(dataset, kge_model) if use_kge_embeddings else (None, None)
        self.num_neg_samples_per_link = num_neg_samples_per_link
        self.file_name = file_name

        ssp_graph, __, __, __, id2entity, id2relation = process_files(raw_data_paths, included_relations)
        self.num_rels = len(ssp_graph)

        # Add transpose matrices to handle both directions of relations.
        if add_traspose_rels:
            ssp_graph_t = [adj.T for adj in ssp_graph]
            ssp_graph += ssp_graph_t

        # the effective number of relations after adding symmetric adjacency matrices and/or self connections
        self.aug_num_rels = len(ssp_graph)
        self.graph = ssp_multigraph_to_dgl(ssp_graph)
        self.ssp_graph = ssp_graph
        self.id2entity = id2entity
        self.id2relation = id2relation

        self.max_n_label = np.array([0, 0])
        with self.main_env.begin() as txn:
            self.max_n_label[0] = int.from_bytes(txn.get('max_n_label_sub'.encode()), byteorder='little')
            self.max_n_label[1] = int.from_bytes(txn.get('max_n_label_obj'.encode()), byteorder='little')

            self.avg_subgraph_size = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('avg_subgraph_size'.encode()))
            self.min_subgraph_size = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('min_subgraph_size'.encode()))
            self.max_subgraph_size = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('max_subgraph_size'.encode()))
            self.std_subgraph_size = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('std_subgraph_size'.encode()))

            self.avg_enc_ratio = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('avg_enc_ratio'.encode()))
            self.min_enc_ratio = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('min_enc_ratio'.encode()))
            self.max_enc_ratio = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('max_enc_ratio'.encode()))
            self.std_enc_ratio = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('std_enc_ratio'.encode()))

            self.avg_num_pruned_nodes = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('avg_num_pruned_nodes'.encode()))
            self.min_num_pruned_nodes = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('min_num_pruned_nodes'.encode()))
            self.max_num_pruned_nodes = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('max_num_pruned_nodes'.encode()))
            self.std_num_pruned_nodes = struct.unpack('f', txn.get('std_num_pruned_nodes'.encode()))

        logging.info(f"Max distance from sub : {self.max_n_label[0]}, Max distance from obj : {self.max_n_label[1]}")

        with self.main_env.begin(db=self.db_pos) as txn:
            self.num_graphs_pos = int.from_bytes(txn.get('num_graphs'.encode()), byteorder='little')
        with self.main_env.begin(db=self.db_neg) as txn:
            self.num_graphs_neg = int.from_bytes(txn.get('num_graphs'.encode()), byteorder='little')

        self.__getitem__(0)

    def __getitem__(self, index):
        with self.main_env.begin(db=self.db_pos) as txn:
            str_id = '{:08}'.format(index).encode('ascii')
            nodes_pos, r_label_pos, g_label_pos, n_labels_pos = deserialize(txn.get(str_id)).values()
            subgraph_pos = self._prepare_subgraphs(nodes_pos, r_label_pos, n_labels_pos)
        subgraphs_neg = []
        r_labels_neg = []
        g_labels_neg = []
        with self.main_env.begin(db=self.db_neg) as txn:
            for i in range(self.num_neg_samples_per_link):
                str_id = '{:08}'.format(index + i * (self.num_graphs_pos)).encode('ascii')
                nodes_neg, r_label_neg, g_label_neg, n_labels_neg = deserialize(txn.get(str_id)).values()
                subgraphs_neg.append(self._prepare_subgraphs(nodes_neg, r_label_neg, n_labels_neg))
                r_labels_neg.append(r_label_neg)
                g_labels_neg.append(g_label_neg)

        return subgraph_pos, g_label_pos, r_label_pos, subgraphs_neg, g_labels_neg, r_labels_neg

    def __len__(self):
        return self.num_graphs_pos

    def _prepare_subgraphs(self, nodes, r_label, n_labels):
        subgraph = dgl.DGLGraph(self.graph.subgraph(nodes))
        subgraph.edata['type'] = self.graph.edata['type'][self.graph.subgraph(nodes).parent_eid]
        subgraph.edata['label'] = torch.tensor(r_label * np.ones(subgraph.edata['type'].shape), dtype=torch.long)

        edges_btw_roots = subgraph.edge_id(0, 1)
        rel_link = np.nonzero(subgraph.edata['type'][edges_btw_roots] == r_label)
        if rel_link.squeeze().nelement() == 0:
            subgraph.add_edge(0, 1)
            subgraph.edata['type'][-1] = torch.tensor(r_label).type(torch.LongTensor)
            subgraph.edata['label'][-1] = torch.tensor(r_label).type(torch.LongTensor)

        # map the id read by GraIL to the entity IDs as registered by the KGE embeddings
        kge_nodes = [self.kge_entity2id[self.id2entity[n]] for n in nodes] if self.kge_entity2id else None
        n_feats = self.node_features[kge_nodes] if self.node_features is not None else None
        subgraph = self._prepare_features_new(subgraph, n_labels, n_feats)

        return subgraph

    def _prepare_features(self, subgraph, n_labels, n_feats=None):
        # One hot encode the node label feature and concat to n_featsure
        n_nodes = subgraph.number_of_nodes()
        label_feats = np.zeros((n_nodes, self.max_n_label[0] + 1))
        label_feats[np.arange(n_nodes), n_labels] = 1
        label_feats[np.arange(n_nodes), self.max_n_label[0] + 1 + n_labels[:, 1]] = 1
        n_feats = np.concatenate((label_feats, n_feats), axis=1) if n_feats else label_feats
        subgraph.ndata['feat'] = torch.FloatTensor(n_feats)
        self.n_feat_dim = n_feats.shape[1]  # Find cleaner way to do this -- i.e. set the n_feat_dim
        return subgraph

    def _prepare_features_new(self, subgraph, n_labels, n_feats=None):
        # One hot encode the node label feature and concat to n_featsure
        n_nodes = subgraph.number_of_nodes()
        label_feats = np.zeros((n_nodes, self.max_n_label[0] + 1 + self.max_n_label[1] + 1))
        label_feats[np.arange(n_nodes), n_labels[:, 0]] = 1
        label_feats[np.arange(n_nodes), self.max_n_label[0] + 1 + n_labels[:, 1]] = 1
        n_feats = np.concatenate((label_feats, n_feats), axis=1) if n_feats is not None else label_feats
        subgraph.ndata['feat'] = torch.FloatTensor(n_feats)

        head_id = np.argwhere([label[0] == 0 and label[1] == 1 for label in n_labels])
        tail_id = np.argwhere([label[0] == 1 and label[1] == 0 for label in n_labels])
        n_ids = np.zeros(n_nodes)
        n_ids[head_id] = 1  # head
        n_ids[tail_id] = 2  # tail
        subgraph.ndata['id'] = torch.FloatTensor(n_ids)

        self.n_feat_dim = n_feats.shape[1]  # Find cleaner way to do this -- i.e. set the n_feat_dim
        return subgraph


# File Path: model/dgl/aggregators.py
import abc
import torch.nn as nn
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Aggregator(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, emb_dim):
        super(Aggregator, self).__init__()

    def forward(self, node):
        curr_emb = node.mailbox['curr_emb'][:, 0, :]  # (B, F)
        nei_msg = torch.bmm(node.mailbox['alpha'].transpose(1, 2), node.mailbox['msg']).squeeze(1)  # (B, F)
        # nei_msg, _ = torch.max(node.mailbox['msg'], 1)  # (B, F)

        new_emb = self.update_embedding(curr_emb, nei_msg)

        return {'h': new_emb}

    @abc.abstractmethod
    def update_embedding(curr_emb, nei_msg):
        raise NotImplementedError


class SumAggregator(Aggregator):
    def __init__(self, emb_dim):
        super(SumAggregator, self).__init__(emb_dim)

    def update_embedding(self, curr_emb, nei_msg):
        new_emb = nei_msg + curr_emb

        return new_emb


class MLPAggregator(Aggregator):
    def __init__(self, emb_dim):
        super(MLPAggregator, self).__init__(emb_dim)
        self.linear = nn.Linear(2 * emb_dim, emb_dim)

    def update_embedding(self, curr_emb, nei_msg):
        inp = torch.cat((nei_msg, curr_emb), 1)
        new_emb = F.relu(self.linear(inp))

        return new_emb


class GRUAggregator(Aggregator):
    def __init__(self, emb_dim):
        super(GRUAggregator, self).__init__(emb_dim)
        self.gru = nn.GRUCell(emb_dim, emb_dim)

    def update_embedding(self, curr_emb, nei_msg):
        new_emb = self.gru(nei_msg, curr_emb)

        return new_emb


# File Path: model/dgl/layers.py
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Identity(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super(Identity, self).__init__()

    def forward(self, x):
        return x


class RGCNLayer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, inp_dim, out_dim, aggregator, bias=None, activation=None, dropout=0.0, edge_dropout=0.0, is_input_layer=False):
        super(RGCNLayer, self).__init__()
        self.bias = bias
        self.activation = activation

        if self.bias:
            self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(out_dim))
            nn.init.xavier_uniform_(self.bias,
                                    gain=nn.init.calculate_gain('relu'))

        self.aggregator = aggregator

        if dropout:
            self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        else:
            self.dropout = None

        if edge_dropout:
            self.edge_dropout = nn.Dropout(edge_dropout)
        else:
            self.edge_dropout = Identity()

    def propagate(self, g):
        raise NotImplementedError

    def forward(self, g, attn_rel_emb=None):

        self.propagate(g, attn_rel_emb)

        # apply bias and activation
        node_repr = g.ndata['h']
        if self.bias:
            node_repr = node_repr + self.bias
        if self.activation:
            node_repr = self.activation(node_repr)
        if self.dropout:
            node_repr = self.dropout(node_repr)

        g.ndata['h'] = node_repr

        if self.is_input_layer:
            g.ndata['repr'] = g.ndata['h'].unsqueeze(1)
        else:
            g.ndata['repr'] = torch.cat([g.ndata['repr'], g.ndata['h'].unsqueeze(1)], dim=1)


class RGCNBasisLayer(RGCNLayer):
    def __init__(self, inp_dim, out_dim, aggregator, attn_rel_emb_dim, num_rels, num_bases=-1, bias=None,
                 activation=None, dropout=0.0, edge_dropout=0.0, is_input_layer=False, has_attn=False):
        super(
            RGCNBasisLayer,
            self).__init__(
            inp_dim,
            out_dim,
            aggregator,
            bias,
            activation,
            dropout=dropout,
            edge_dropout=edge_dropout,
            is_input_layer=is_input_layer)
        self.inp_dim = inp_dim
        self.out_dim = out_dim
        self.attn_rel_emb_dim = attn_rel_emb_dim
        self.num_rels = num_rels
        self.num_bases = num_bases
        self.is_input_layer = is_input_layer
        self.has_attn = has_attn

        if self.num_bases <= 0 or self.num_bases > self.num_rels:
            self.num_bases = self.num_rels

        # add basis weights
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(self.num_bases, self.inp_dim, self.out_dim))
        self.w_comp = nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(self.num_rels, self.num_bases))

        if self.has_attn:
            self.A = nn.Linear(2 * self.inp_dim + 2 * self.attn_rel_emb_dim, inp_dim)
            self.B = nn.Linear(inp_dim, 1)

        self.self_loop_weight = nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(self.inp_dim, self.out_dim))

        nn.init.xavier_uniform_(self.self_loop_weight, gain=nn.init.calculate_gain('relu'))
        nn.init.xavier_uniform_(self.weight, gain=nn.init.calculate_gain('relu'))
        nn.init.xavier_uniform_(self.w_comp, gain=nn.init.calculate_gain('relu'))

    def propagate(self, g, attn_rel_emb=None):
        # generate all weights from bases
        weight = self.weight.view(self.num_bases,
                                  self.inp_dim * self.out_dim)
        weight = torch.matmul(self.w_comp, weight).view(
            self.num_rels, self.inp_dim, self.out_dim)

        g.edata['w'] = self.edge_dropout(torch.ones(g.number_of_edges(), 1).to(weight.device))

        input_ = 'feat' if self.is_input_layer else 'h'

        def msg_func(edges):
            w = weight.index_select(0, edges.data['type'])
            msg = edges.data['w'] * torch.bmm(edges.src[input_].unsqueeze(1), w).squeeze(1)
            curr_emb = torch.mm(edges.dst[input_], self.self_loop_weight)  # (B, F)

            if self.has_attn:
                e = torch.cat([edges.src[input_], edges.dst[input_], attn_rel_emb(edges.data['type']), attn_rel_emb(edges.data['label'])], dim=1)
                a = torch.sigmoid(self.B(F.relu(self.A(e))))
            else:
                a = torch.ones((len(edges), 1)).to(device=w.device)

            return {'curr_emb': curr_emb, 'msg': msg, 'alpha': a}

        g.update_all(msg_func, self.aggregator, None)


# File Path: model/dgl/rgcn_model.py
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from .layers import RGCNBasisLayer as RGCNLayer

from .aggregators import SumAggregator, MLPAggregator, GRUAggregator


class RGCN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, params):
        super(RGCN, self).__init__()

        self.max_label_value = params.max_label_value
        self.inp_dim = params.inp_dim
        self.emb_dim = params.emb_dim
        self.attn_rel_emb_dim = params.attn_rel_emb_dim
        self.num_rels = params.num_rels
        self.aug_num_rels = params.aug_num_rels
        self.num_bases = params.num_bases
        self.num_hidden_layers = params.num_gcn_layers
        self.dropout = params.dropout
        self.edge_dropout = params.edge_dropout
        self.has_attn = params.has_attn

        self.device = params.device

        if self.has_attn:
            self.attn_rel_emb = nn.Embedding(self.num_rels, self.attn_rel_emb_dim, sparse=False)
        else:
            self.attn_rel_emb = None

        # initialize aggregators for input and hidden layers
        if params.gnn_agg_type == "sum":
            self.aggregator = SumAggregator(self.emb_dim)
        elif params.gnn_agg_type == "mlp":
            self.aggregator = MLPAggregator(self.emb_dim)
        elif params.gnn_agg_type == "gru":
            self.aggregator = GRUAggregator(self.emb_dim)

        # create rgcn layers
        self.build_model()

        # create initial features
        self.features = self.create_features()

    def create_features(self):
        features = torch.arange(self.inp_dim).to(device=self.device)
        return features

    def build_model(self):
        self.layers = nn.ModuleList()
        # i2h
        i2h = self.build_input_layer()
        if i2h is not None:
            self.layers.append(i2h)
        # h2h
        for idx in range(self.num_hidden_layers - 1):
            h2h = self.build_hidden_layer(idx)
            self.layers.append(h2h)

    def build_input_layer(self):
        return RGCNLayer(self.inp_dim,
                         self.emb_dim,
                         self.aggregator,
                         self.attn_rel_emb_dim,
                         self.aug_num_rels,
                         self.num_bases,
                         activation=F.relu,
                         dropout=self.dropout,
                         edge_dropout=self.edge_dropout,
                         is_input_layer=True,
                         has_attn=self.has_attn)

    def build_hidden_layer(self, idx):
        return RGCNLayer(self.emb_dim,
                         self.emb_dim,
                         self.aggregator,
                         self.attn_rel_emb_dim,
                         self.aug_num_rels,
                         self.num_bases,
                         activation=F.relu,
                         dropout=self.dropout,
                         edge_dropout=self.edge_dropout,
                         has_attn=self.has_attn)

    def forward(self, g):
        for layer in self.layers:
            layer(g, self.attn_rel_emb)
        return g.ndata.pop('h')


# File Path: model/dgl/graph_classifier.py
from .rgcn_model import RGCN
from dgl import mean_nodes
import torch.nn as nn
import torch


class GraphClassifier(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, params, relation2id):
        super().__init__()

        self.params = params
        self.relation2id = relation2id

        self.gnn = RGCN(params)
        self.rel_emb = nn.Embedding(self.params.num_rels, self.params.rel_emb_dim, sparse=False)

        if self.params.add_ht_emb:
            self.fc_layer = nn.Linear(3 * self.params.num_gcn_layers * self.params.emb_dim + self.params.rel_emb_dim, 1)
        else:
            self.fc_layer = nn.Linear(self.params.num_gcn_layers * self.params.emb_dim + self.params.rel_emb_dim, 1)

    def forward(self, data):
        g, rel_labels = data
        g.ndata['h'] = self.gnn(g)

        g_out = mean_nodes(g, 'repr')

        head_ids = (g.ndata['id'] == 1).nonzero().squeeze(1)
        head_embs = g.ndata['repr'][head_ids]

        tail_ids = (g.ndata['id'] == 2).nonzero().squeeze(1)
        tail_embs = g.ndata['repr'][tail_ids]

        if self.params.add_ht_emb:
            g_rep = torch.cat([g_out.view(-1, self.params.num_gcn_layers * self.params.emb_dim),
                               head_embs.view(-1, self.params.num_gcn_layers * self.params.emb_dim),
                               tail_embs.view(-1, self.params.num_gcn_layers * self.params.emb_dim),
                               self.rel_emb(rel_labels)], dim=1)
        else:
            g_rep = torch.cat([g_out.view(-1, self.params.num_gcn_layers * self.params.emb_dim), self.rel_emb(rel_labels)], dim=1)

        output = self.fc_layer(g_rep)
        return output


# File Path: managers/trainer.py
import statistics
import timeit
import os
import logging
import pdb
import numpy as np
import time

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

from sklearn import metrics


class Trainer():
    def __init__(self, params, graph_classifier, train, valid_evaluator=None):
        self.graph_classifier = graph_classifier
        self.valid_evaluator = valid_evaluator
        self.params = params
        self.train_data = train

        self.updates_counter = 0

        model_params = list(self.graph_classifier.parameters())
        logging.info('Total number of parameters: %d' % sum(map(lambda x: x.numel(), model_params)))

        if params.optimizer == "SGD":
            self.optimizer = optim.SGD(model_params, lr=params.lr, momentum=params.momentum, weight_decay=self.params.l2)
        if params.optimizer == "Adam":
            self.optimizer = optim.Adam(model_params, lr=params.lr, weight_decay=self.params.l2)

        self.criterion = nn.MarginRankingLoss(self.params.margin, reduction='sum')

        self.reset_training_state()

    def reset_training_state(self):
        self.best_metric = 0
        self.last_metric = 0
        self.not_improved_count = 0

    def train_epoch(self):
        total_loss = 0
        all_preds = []
        all_labels = []
        all_scores = []

        dataloader = DataLoader(self.train_data, batch_size=self.params.batch_size, shuffle=True, num_workers=self.params.num_workers, collate_fn=self.params.collate_fn)
        self.graph_classifier.train()
        model_params = list(self.graph_classifier.parameters())
        for b_idx, batch in enumerate(dataloader):
            data_pos, targets_pos, data_neg, targets_neg = self.params.move_batch_to_device(batch, self.params.device)
            self.optimizer.zero_grad()
            score_pos = self.graph_classifier(data_pos)
            score_neg = self.graph_classifier(data_neg)
            loss = self.criterion(score_pos, score_neg.view(len(score_pos), -1).mean(dim=1), torch.Tensor([1]).to(device=self.params.device))
            loss.backward()
            self.optimizer.step()
            self.updates_counter += 1

            with torch.no_grad():
                all_scores += score_pos.squeeze().detach().cpu().tolist() + score_neg.squeeze().detach().cpu().tolist()
                all_labels += targets_pos.tolist() + targets_neg.tolist()
                total_loss += loss

            if self.valid_evaluator and self.params.eval_every_iter and self.updates_counter % self.params.eval_every_iter == 0:
                tic = time.time()
                result = self.valid_evaluator.eval()
                logging.info('\nPerformance:' + str(result) + 'in ' + str(time.time() - tic))

                if result['auc'] >= self.best_metric:
                    self.save_classifier()
                    self.best_metric = result['auc']
                    self.not_improved_count = 0

                else:
                    self.not_improved_count += 1
                    if self.not_improved_count > self.params.early_stop:
                        logging.info(f"Validation performance didn\'t improve for {self.params.early_stop} epochs. Training stops.")
                        break
                self.last_metric = result['auc']

        auc = metrics.roc_auc_score(all_labels, all_scores)
        auc_pr = metrics.average_precision_score(all_labels, all_scores)

        weight_norm = sum(map(lambda x: torch.norm(x), model_params))

        return total_loss, auc, auc_pr, weight_norm

    def train(self):
        self.reset_training_state()

        for epoch in range(1, self.params.num_epochs + 1):
            time_start = time.time()
            loss, auc, auc_pr, weight_norm = self.train_epoch()
            time_elapsed = time.time() - time_start
            logging.info(f'Epoch {epoch} with loss: {loss}, training auc: {auc}, training auc_pr: {auc_pr}, best validation AUC: {self.best_metric}, weight_norm: {weight_norm} in {time_elapsed}')

            if epoch % self.params.save_every == 0:
                torch.save(self.graph_classifier, os.path.join(self.params.exp_dir, 'graph_classifier_chk.pth'))

    def save_classifier(self):
        torch.save(self.graph_classifier, os.path.join(self.params.exp_dir, 'best_graph_classifier.pth'))
        logging.info('Better models found w.r.t accuracy. Saved it!')

import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import pdb
from sklearn import metrics
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader


class Evaluator():
    def __init__(self, params, graph_classifier, data):
        self.params = params
        self.graph_classifier = graph_classifier
        self.data = data

    def eval(self, save=False):
        pos_scores = []
        pos_labels = []
        neg_scores = []
        neg_labels = []
        dataloader = DataLoader(self.data, batch_size=self.params.batch_size, shuffle=False, num_workers=self.params.num_workers, collate_fn=self.params.collate_fn)

        self.graph_classifier.eval()
        with torch.no_grad():
            for b_idx, batch in enumerate(dataloader):

                data_pos, targets_pos, data_neg, targets_neg = self.params.move_batch_to_device(batch, self.params.device)
                score_pos = self.graph_classifier(data_pos)
                score_neg = self.graph_classifier(data_neg)

                pos_scores += score_pos.squeeze(1).detach().cpu().tolist()
                neg_scores += score_neg.squeeze(1).detach().cpu().tolist()
                pos_labels += targets_pos.tolist()
                neg_labels += targets_neg.tolist()

        auc = metrics.roc_auc_score(pos_labels + neg_labels, pos_scores + neg_scores)
        auc_pr = metrics.average_precision_score(pos_labels + neg_labels, pos_scores + neg_scores)

        if save:
            pos_test_triplets_path = os.path.join(self.params.main_dir, 'data/{}/{}.txt'.format(self.params.dataset, self.data.file_name))
            with open(pos_test_triplets_path) as f:
                pos_triplets = [line.split() for line in f.read().split('\n')[:-1]]
            pos_file_path = os.path.join(self.params.main_dir, 'data/{}/grail_{}_predictions.txt'.format(self.params.dataset, self.data.file_name))
            with open(pos_file_path, "w") as f:
                for ([s, r, o], score) in zip(pos_triplets, pos_scores):
                    f.write('\t'.join([s, r, o, str(score)]) + '\n')

            neg_test_triplets_path = os.path.join(self.params.main_dir, 'data/{}/neg_{}_0.txt'.format(self.params.dataset, self.data.file_name))
            with open(neg_test_triplets_path) as f:
                neg_triplets = [line.split() for line in f.read().split('\n')[:-1]]
            neg_file_path = os.path.join(self.params.main_dir, 'data/{}/grail_neg_{}_{}_predictions.txt'.format(self.params.dataset, self.data.file_name, self.params.constrained_neg_prob))
            with open(neg_file_path, "w") as f:
                for ([s, r, o], score) in zip(neg_triplets, neg_scores):
                    f.write('\t'.join([s, r, o, str(score)]) + '\n')

        return {'auc': auc, 'auc_pr': auc_pr}


# File Path: utils/graph_utils.py
import statistics
import numpy as np
import scipy.sparse as ssp
import torch
import networkx as nx
import dgl
import pickle


def serialize(data):
    data_tuple = tuple(data.values())
    return pickle.dumps(data_tuple)


def deserialize(data):
    data_tuple = pickle.loads(data)
    keys = ('nodes', 'r_label', 'g_label', 'n_label')
    return dict(zip(keys, data_tuple))


def get_edge_count(adj_list):
    count = []
    for adj in adj_list:
        count.append(len(adj.tocoo().row.tolist()))
    return np.array(count)


def incidence_matrix(adj_list):
    rows, cols, dats = [], [], []
    dim = adj_list[0].shape
    for adj in adj_list:
        adjcoo = adj.tocoo()
        rows += adjcoo.row.tolist()
        cols += adjcoo.col.tolist()
        dats += adjcoo.data.tolist()
    row = np.array(rows)
    col = np.array(cols)
    data = np.array(dats)
    return ssp.csc_matrix((data, (row, col)), shape=dim)


def ssp_multigraph_to_dgl(graph, n_feats=None):
    g_nx = nx.MultiDiGraph()
    g_nx.add_nodes_from(list(range(graph[0].shape[0])))
    for rel, adj in enumerate(graph):
        nx_triplets = []
        for src, dst in list(zip(adj.tocoo().row, adj.tocoo().col)):
            nx_triplets.append((src, dst, {'type': rel}))
        g_nx.add_edges_from(nx_triplets)

    g_dgl = dgl.DGLGraph(multigraph=True)
    g_dgl.from_networkx(g_nx, edge_attrs=['type'])
    if n_feats is not None:
        g_dgl.ndata['feat'] = torch.tensor(n_feats)

    return g_dgl


def collate_dgl(samples):
    graphs_pos, g_labels_pos, r_labels_pos, graphs_negs, g_labels_negs, r_labels_negs = map(list, zip(*samples))
    batched_graph_pos = dgl.batch(graphs_pos)

    graphs_neg = [item for sublist in graphs_negs for item in sublist]
    g_labels_neg = [item for sublist in g_labels_negs for item in sublist]
    r_labels_neg = [item for sublist in r_labels_negs for item in sublist]

    batched_graph_neg = dgl.batch(graphs_neg)
    return (batched_graph_pos, r_labels_pos), g_labels_pos, (batched_graph_neg, r_labels_neg), g_labels_neg


def move_batch_to_device_dgl(batch, device):
    ((g_dgl_pos, r_labels_pos), targets_pos, (g_dgl_neg, r_labels_neg), targets_neg) = batch

    targets_pos = torch.LongTensor(targets_pos).to(device=device)
    r_labels_pos = torch.LongTensor(r_labels_pos).to(device=device)

    targets_neg = torch.LongTensor(targets_neg).to(device=device)
    r_labels_neg = torch.LongTensor(r_labels_neg).to(device=device)

    g_dgl_pos = send_graph_to_device(g_dgl_pos, device)
    g_dgl_neg = send_graph_to_device(g_dgl_neg, device)

    return ((g_dgl_pos, r_labels_pos), targets_pos, (g_dgl_neg, r_labels_neg), targets_neg)


def send_graph_to_device(g, device):
    labels = g.node_attr_schemes()
    for l in labels.keys():
        g.ndata[l] = g.ndata.pop(l).to(device)

    labels = g.edge_attr_schemes()
    for l in labels.keys():
        g.edata[l] = g.edata.pop(l).to(device)
    return g

                            kge_model=params.kge_model, file_name=params.train_file)
    valid = SubgraphDataset(params.db_path, 'valid_pos', 'valid_neg', params.file_paths,
                            add_traspose_rels=params.add_traspose_rels,
                            num_neg_samples_per_link=params.num_neg_samples_per_link,
                            use_kge_embeddings=params.use_kge_embeddings, dataset=params.dataset,
                            kge_model=params.kge_model, file_name=params.valid_file)

    params.num_rels = train.num_rels
    params.aug_num_rels = train.aug_num_rels
    params.inp_dim = train.n_feat_dim

    params.max_label_value = train.max_n_label

    graph_classifier = initialize_model(params, dgl_model, params.load_model)

    logging.info(f"Device: {params.device}")
    logging.info(f"Input dim : {params.inp_dim}, # Relations : {params.num_rels}, # Augmented relations : {params.aug_num_rels}")

    valid_evaluator = Evaluator(params, graph_classifier, valid)

    trainer = Trainer(params, graph_classifier, train, valid_evaluator)

    logging.info('Starting training with full batch...')

    trainer.train()
llm_extracted_info.experimental_info:
Core method implementation (GraIL):
- Enclosing subgraph extraction (edge directions ignored during extraction):
  - Code: subgraph_extraction/graph_sampler.py
    - subgraph_extraction_labeling(): builds incidence matrix over all relations, symmetrizes it (A + A^T), collects k-hop neighborhoods from head and tail, and extracts nodes on the union or intersection depending on params.enclosing_sub_graph. Labels nodes via DRNL (node_label), and prunes nodes with max distance > hop. Returns pruned nodes, DRNL labels, stats.
    - node_label(): Double-Radius Vertex Labeling. For roots [0,1], removes each root and computes shortest-path distances to the other; assigns labels as (d(i,u), d(i,v)). Targets have labels (0,1) and (1,0).
    - sample_neg(): negative sampling by replacing head or tail, optionally constrained to valid heads/tails for relation with probability params.constrained_neg_prob.
    - links2subgraphs(): extracts and LMDB-stores subgraphs for pos/neg links; accumulates label caps and statistics.
- Node labeling features and target-node flags:
  - Code: subgraph_extraction/datasets.py
    - SubgraphDataset._prepare_features_new(): one-hot of d(i,u) in [0..max_sub] and d(i,v) in [0..max_obj], concatenated; adds nd: 'id' where head node id=1, tail id=2; stores per-layer representations later used for JK-like concatenation.
    - SubgraphDataset._prepare_subgraphs(): builds DGL subgraph with edge attributes: 'type' (relation id), 'label' (target relation r_t on every edge), and ensures an explicit 0->1 edge of type r_t exists.
- GNN scoring with relation-specific weights, attention, and edge dropout:
  - Code: model/dgl/layers.py (RGCNBasisLayer) and model/dgl/aggregators.py
    - Basis decomposition: self.weight ∈ R[B, Din, Dout], w_comp ∈ R[R, B] → per-relation W_r.
    - Message passing uses DGL update_all with custom message fn:
      msg = edge_dropout_weight * (h_src W_{type}). Self-loop via learned self_loop_weight applied to h_dst becomes curr_emb. Edge attention α = sigmoid(B(ReLU(A([h_src, h_dst, E(type), E(label)])))) when has_attn=True, where E(·) are learned embeddings of edge relation type r and target relation r_t (attn_rel_emb). Aggregation uses chosen aggregator (sum, MLP, or GRU) over messages weighted by α. After each layer: bias, ReLU, dropout applied.
    - Edge dropout: per-edge multiplicative mask g.edata['w'] from nn.Dropout applied to ones.
  - JK-style connections: model/dgl/layers.py stores g.ndata['repr'] as concatenation across layers (input plus hidden), enabling downstream concatenation.
  - Code: model/dgl/rgcn_model.py builds stack of L= params.num_gcn_layers RGCN layers with shared aggregator type, basis count, and attention option; attn_rel_emb provides relation embeddings to attention.
- Subgraph representation and final scoring:
  - Code: model/dgl/graph_classifier.py
    - After GNN, mean_nodes pooling over g.ndata['repr'] to get subgraph representation; optionally concatenates per-target head and tail representations (identified by node id flags) and a learned embedding of the target relation r_t; final linear layer outputs scalar score.
- Training objective and negative sampling:
  - Code: managers/trainer.py and subgraph_extraction/graph_sampler.py
    - MarginRankingLoss with margin=params.margin between positive score and mean negative scores per positive. Mini-batch training via torch DataLoader and custom collate.
    - Negatives: For each positive link, sample params.num_neg_samples_per_link links by replacing head or tail while ensuring non-existing edges; constrained_neg_prob optionally forces replacements among observed heads/tails per relation.
- Data collation and GPU movement:
  - Code: utils/graph_utils.py
    - collate_dgl(): batches positive graphs and flattens negatives across samples; also returns relation labels for edges in batched graphs.
    - move_batch_to_device_dgl(): moves batched DGL graphs and labels to device.

Default experimental settings (from train.py argparse defaults):
- Data and subgraph extraction:
  - hop: 3 (k-hop for DRNL/enclosing subgraphs)
  - enclosing_sub_graph: True (intersection of k-hop neighborhoods)
  - max_nodes_per_hop: None (no subsampling)
  - add_traspose_rels: False (message passing uses directed multi-graph; extraction uses undirected incidence internally)
  - num_neg_samples_per_link: 1
  - constrained_neg_prob: 0.0 (pure head/tail replacement uniformly among all entities)
- Model (GNN):
  - num_gcn_layers: 3
  - emb_dim: 32 (hidden size per layer)
  - num_bases: 4 (basis decomposition for relation-specific weights)
  - gnn_agg_type: 'sum' (choose among 'sum' | 'mlp' | 'gru')
  - has_attn: True (use sigmoid gate α using source/target states, edge type r, target relation r_t)
  - dropout: 0.0 (node representation dropout per layer)
  - edge_dropout: 0.5 (message dropout via edge weights)
  - rel_emb_dim: 32 (for final classifier)
  - attn_rel_emb_dim: 32 (for attention gate inputs E(r), E(r_t))
  - add_ht_emb: True (concatenate head/tail node representations from all layers with pooled subgraph and relation embedding before scoring)
  - JK connections: enabled by concatenating per-layer node embeddings in g.ndata['repr'] (implicit; used by classifier)
- Training:
  - optimizer: Adam
  - lr: 0.01
  - l2 (weight decay): 5e-4
  - batch_size: 16
  - num_workers: 8
  - num_epochs: 100
  - margin (MarginRankingLoss): 10
  - early_stop patience (checked via eval_every_iter): 100
  - eval_every_iter: 455 (periodic validation and checkpointing)
  - save_every epochs: 10
- Device handling:
  - CUDA selected via --gpu unless --disable_cuda.
- Files/paths:
  - Training/validation files: data/{dataset}/train.txt and valid.txt.
  - Subgraphs cached to LMDB under data/{dataset}/subgraphs_en_{enclosing}_neg_{neg}_hop_{hop}.
  - Relation-to-id mapping persisted at data/{dataset}/relation2id.json.
- Evaluation (AUC):
  - Evaluator computes ROC-AUC and Average Precision over batched pos/neg subgraphs; can save per-triplet scores to data/{dataset}/grail_[split]_predictions.txt; also saves negatives to grail_neg_[split]_[constrained_neg_prob]_predictions.txt.

Notes on implementation details aligning with the method description:
- Extraction ignores edge directions: subgraph_extraction_labeling uses incidence_matrix over relations and symmetrizes it (A_incidence + A_incidence.T) before BFS to compute neighborhoods.
- Attention gate α depends on h_src, h_dst, edge type r (edges.data['type']), and target relation r_t (edges.data['label']), via learned embeddings attn_rel_emb and a 2-layer MLP with sigmoid output.
- Relation-specific weights with basis decomposition: RGCNBasisLayer generates W_r per relation from basis weights and composition coefficients.
- Self-loops: each layer applies a learned self_loop_weight on destination node state and combines with aggregated neighbor messages via the chosen aggregator.
- JK-like concatenation: g.ndata['repr'] accumulates per-layer node states; classifier concatenates these for pooled graph, head, and tail representations before final linear scoring.
- Negative sampling: replaces head or tail while ensuring the sampled (h', r, t') is not an existing edge for relation r.

new_method.method:
Title: REA-Cycle++: Relation‑Equivariant, Invertible Adapters with Group/Composition Constraints to Cure the Reversal Curse

Motivation
- Why this matters now (リバーサルカースの克服): CE fine‑tuning learns direction‑specific decoder rows, so A→B does not imply B←A or A—>>C. Theory (training dynamics) diagnoses row asymmetry; practice shows ICL works and many relations are near‑linear in a low‑dim subspace (LREs). There is a clear opportunity to inject an explicit, invertible, relation‑aware head that converts forward‑only supervision into reverse and multi‑hop competence without harming fluency.
- Gap: Prior work either diagnoses (training dynamics), prompts around (ICL/CoT), or augments data. LREs analyze/patch post‑hoc; no light‑weight, in‑training, invertible module inside the next‑token objective that (i) localizes to entity decoding, (ii) enforces inverse/closure from forward‑only labels, (iii) preserves base grammar, and (iv) yields transparent geometry with auditable constraints.

Methodology
High‑level idea
- Add a tiny, relation‑conditioned, invertible adapter active only on entity tokens. It operates in a learned k‑dim entity subspace and is trained with forward labels plus inverse, cycle/composition, and symmetric‑distribution constraints. Directionality is moved from decoder rows into an explicit invertible head; base fluency is preserved via gating and KL‑to‑base.

Architectural components (drop‑in for existing GPT2CustomLMHeadModel_logits)
- Entity subspace and table
  - P∈R^{d×k}: projector from last‑layer hidden h_t to z=P^T h_t (k≪d). Light orthogonality/spectral norm regularization; PCA init on entity states.
  - E∈R^{|V_ent|×k}: entity matrix (projected input embeddings times a random orthogonal rotation), trained with small LR.
- Relation maps with strict invertibility and compositionality
  - Lie-parametrized orthogonal core: M_r=exp(A_r), with A_r skew‑symmetric (A_r=−A_r^T). Efficient parameterization via Householder/Cayley or matrix‑exponential of a low‑rank skew sum. Guarantees invertibility and stable composition.
  - Mild expressivity: spectrally‑bounded residual R_r (||R_r||2≤ε) and Ĥ_r=(I+R_r)M_r; keeps invertible while modeling deviations from perfect linearity.
  - Inverses and compositions by construction: M_{r^{-1}}=M_r^T; composed head M_{r∘s}=M_s M_r; registry constructs composed scorers on the fly.
- Scoring, mixing, and compute control
  - Adapter logits (entity positions only): l_adapter=(E Ĥ_r z)/τ_r + b_r, with per‑relation temperature τ_r and bias b_r for calibration.
  - Mixture with base: l_mix=(1−α) l_base + α l_adapter. Gating α=g(h_t)∈[0,1] via a small MLP; schedule from α≈1 on entity positions to α_min, and entropy/energy gating at inference (activate only when confident). Top‑K pruning: score only base top‑K candidates to keep O(kK).
- Optional decoder factorization (safer edits)
  - Row‑localization: factor entity‑rows of W_out as W_base + U P^T and learn U only; non‑entity rows frozen. Restricts edits to the intended slice and eases auditing.

Training objectives (no reverse labels required)
- Forward CE: standard CE on seen directions using l_mix. Freeze transformer; train adapter first; optionally unfreeze lm_head late with tiny LR.
- Alignment (contrastive): InfoNCE/cosine‑margin to align Ĥ_→ z_A with E_B (and implicitly Ĥ_← z_B with E_A via inverse tying); in‑batch negatives; temperatures share τ_r.
- Exact symmetry and invertibility: enforce ||M_{r^{-1}}M_r−I||_F^2, ||M_r^T M_r−I||_F^2; bound ||R_r||_2 (spectral penalty) to keep Ĥ_r invertible.
- Composition (cycle) constraints: on ChainDataset batches (A→B, B→C), minimize ||Ĥ_→(Ĥ_→ z_A)−z_C||^2; and score A—>>C with M_→M_→. Extend to heterogeneous chains r∘s.
- Symmetric distributional consistency (reverse without labels): minimize symmetric KL between p_adapter(.|A→) and swap(p_adapter(.|B←)); match p(.|A—>>) with composed p(.|A→→C).
- LRE/Jacobian distillation (bridging theory and practice): estimate relation Jacobians from the frozen base (few‑shot LRE) or a prompted ICL teacher and minimize ||log M_r − Skew(J_r)||_F (projected to skew space). This transfers known low‑dim linear relational structure into weights.
- Fluency and trust region: KL(l_mix || l_base) on non‑entity positions and a small trust‑region penalty ||l_mix−l_base||^2 at entity positions to limit drift; L2 on gate to reduce overuse; isotropy/whitening of z for stable metrics.

Multi‑token entities and triggering
- Trigger via arrow tokens (synthetic) or a small relation classifier/templater (natural); identify entity spans via lexicon/NER; pool span states to z and score first token (copy‑attention or span distribution for the rest).

Calibration and safety
- Learn τ_r per relation (or global τ) to match base logit scale; entropy/energy gating decides when to apply the adapter. Toggle‑off recovers base behavior.

Implementation plan (concrete hooks into the provided codebase)
- New module: model/AdapterEntityRelHead.py
  - class AdapterEntityRelHead(P, E, {A_r}, {R_r}, {τ_r}, {b_r}, gate)
  - forward(hidden h_t, relation_id, candidate_ids=None) → adapter logits on candidate_ids; supports composed relation ids via runtime products M_s M_r.
- Wire‑in: model/GPT2CustomModel_logits.py
  - In GPT2CustomLMHeadModel.forward, detect entity positions; call adapter; mix logits l_mix and return. Keep base behavior elsewhere. Add top‑K pruning using l_base.
- Training scripts
  - Extend scripts/train_reverse_logits.py and scripts/train_chain_logits.py with λ weights for losses (λ_align, λ_inv, λ_comp, λ_sym, λ_gram, λ_res), gate schedule (α from 1→α_min over 30–50% epochs), options for residual R_r and τ_r, and an LRE‑distill toggle. Initialize P via PCA; E via projected W_in[entities] times random orthogonal; M_→ via orthogonal Procrustes; τ_r via base calibration; default R_r=0.

How this differs from prior work
- Beyond diagnostics (training dynamics): proposes a concrete architectural/training intervention—an invertible, relation‑aware head inside next‑token decoding—rather than analysis or prompting.
- Beyond ICL/data augmentation: learns inverse/closure from forward‑only labels using invertibility, composition, and symmetric‑KL constraints; ICL is optional as a distillation teacher.
- Beyond LREs: uses LRE only as a weak teacher; the core is a train‑time, group‑constrained, invertible map with gating, not post‑hoc linearization/editing.
- Unrelated to SymILO/MSR: those optimize label permutations or parameter sharing in other domains; here we add a minimal, orthogonality‑constrained decoding adapter for token‑level LMs.
- Different from GraIL/RE: operates within next‑token generation, not graph‑subgraph reasoning.

Evaluation (synthetic → natural)
- Synthetic (existing repo): ReverseDataset_logits and ChainDataset_logits.
  - Metrics: forward vs reverse accuracy, reverse gap on unseen pairs, composition error, cycle error ||M_{r^{-1}}M_r−I||, symmetric‑KL residuals, grammar perplexity, gate activation rates, adapter ablations.
  - Ablations: k∈{16,32,64}, α_min, top‑K, Householder vs Cayley vs exp(skew), with/without R_r, with/without L_sym/LRE‑distill, τ_r per‑relation vs global.
- Natural: Wikidata parent↔child, morphology (singular↔plural; tense), bilingual lexicons (en↔es) in short templates.
  - Report reversal parity, two‑hop kinship, BLEU for direction swap in morphology, perplexity on distractor text, and small human grammaticality check.

Expected impact
- Cure reversal and intransitivity with forward‑only labels: close seen–unseen gaps on reverse and chain tasks with tiny parameter overhead and negligible fluency loss; reduce dependence on CoT/prompt hacks and explicit reverse data.
- Compositionality without CoT: Explicit Ĥ_→∘Ĥ_→ and Ĥ_{r∘s} enable two‑hop and heterogeneous chains.
- Interpretability and auditing: P, E, and {M_r} expose a low‑dim relational geometry; spectra, τ_r, and cycle errors are simple diagnostics; the gate quantifies reliance on the adapter.
- Practicality: Model‑agnostic, compute‑light (entity positions; top‑K), plug‑and‑play; can be disabled to recover base behavior; usable for finetuning GPT‑2‑style stacks.
- Bridge ICL and finetuning: Optional LRE/ICL distillation transfers symmetry into weights, improving robustness at temperature 0 and with short prompts.

experimental_design.experiment_strategy:
REA-Cycle++: Experimental Plans and Python-Oriented Implementation Sketches

Common setup (applies to all experiments)
- Base model: HuggingFace GPT-2 small (124M) or Pythia-70M for speed. Tokenizer augmented with special entity tokens and relation markers (e.g., ->, <-, >> for two-hop).
- Freeze transformer weights for the first 80% of training; optionally unfreeze lm_head with tiny LR at the end.
- Adapter hyperparams (default):
  - k=32 (entity subspace); projector P∈R^{d×k} initialized by PCA on entity token hidden states.
  - Entity matrix E: projected embedding matrix for entity vocab times a random orthogonal matrix.
  - Orthogonal core per relation: M_r=exp(A_r) with A_r skew-symmetric from raw B via A=(B−B^T)/2.
  - Residual: R_r with spectral norm penalty; default off (R_r=0) or ε=0.1.
  - Gate g(h_t): small 2-layer MLP; schedule α: linearly decay from 1.0 to α_min=0.35 over first 40% of epochs for entity positions; non-entity α=0.0.
  - Top-K candidate scoring: K=200 by default (from base logits).
  - Temperatures: learn τ_r per relation; initialize to match base logit scale.
- Losses (weights λ):
  - Forward CE: 1.0
  - Alignment (InfoNCE/cos-margin): 0.2
  - Orthogonality/invertibility: 0.1
  - Composition (cycle) constraints: 0.2
  - Symmetric-KL: 0.05
  - Trust region (||l_mix−l_base||^2 on entity positions): 0.05; KL(l_mix || l_base) on non-entity: 0.1
  - Gate L2: 1e-4
  - Optional LRE/Jacobian distill: 0.1
- Optimization: AdamW, LR 5e-4 for adapter params, 5e-5 for E and τ_r, 5e-6 for lm_head when unfrozen; batch size 64; 3–10 epochs depending on dataset size.
- Evaluation metrics (per experiment):
  - Forward accuracy; Reverse accuracy on unseen pairs; Reverse gap (forward minus reverse); Two-hop/chain accuracy; Composition error; Cycle error ||M_{r^{-1}}M_r−I||_F; Symmetric-KL residual; Perplexity on distractor text; Gate activation rate; Runtime overhead; Ablation deltas.

Python scaffolding (shared)
- Core module classes (minimal sketch):
  class AdapterEntityRelHead(torch.nn.Module):
      def __init__(self, d_model, k, n_entities, relation_ids, entity_id_list):
          # P, E, per-relation (B_r -> A_r = (B_r - B_r.T)/2), optional R_r, tau_r, bias, gate MLP
      def orthogonal_core(self, rel):
          A = (self.B[rel] - self.B[rel].T) * 0.5
          M = torch.matrix_exp(A)
          if self.use_residual:
              R = self.R[rel]
              # clip spectral norm via power iteration or register penalty
              M = (torch.eye(self.k, device=M.device) + R) @ M
          return M
      def forward(self, h_t, rel_id, candidate_ids=None):
          z = h_t @ self.P  # z in R^k
          M = self.orthogonal_core(rel_id)
          z_rel = z @ M.T  # shape (B, k)
          E_sub = self.E[candidate_ids] if candidate_ids is not None else self.E
          l_adapter = (z_rel @ E_sub.T) / self.tau[rel_id] + self.bias[rel_id]
          alpha = torch.sigmoid(self.gate(h_t))
          return l_adapter, alpha

  def mix_logits(l_base, l_adapter, alpha):
      return (1 - alpha) * l_base + alpha * l_adapter

- Loss utilities: align loss (InfoNCE over in-batch negatives), symmetric-KL between adapter distributions with relation swapped, invertibility and orthogonality penalties, composition loss using composed cores M_s @ M_r.
- Entity span handling: Use simple lexicon-based tagging for synthetic; NER for natural; pool last-layer states over span (mean) and score first token.


Experiment 1: Synthetic Reversal and Composition (ReverseDataset_logits, ChainDataset_logits)
Goal
- Show REA-Cycle++ converts forward-only supervision into reverse and two-hop competence, while preserving fluency and remaining efficient.

Data
- Entities: 5k synthetic entities with tokens like <E0001> … <E5000>; add to tokenizer as single tokens.
- Relations: r in {R1, R2} (e.g., parent_of, capital_of), each a random bijection π_r over entities. Include a chain dataset for R1∘R1 two-hop.
- Train/test splits:
  - Train: forward pairs (A R1-> B) for 3k entities; reverse pairs excluded. Provide chains A->B, B->C but not A->->C labels.
  - Test: reverse (B R1<- A) over held-out pairs; two-hop A R1>> C; also forward on unseen entities to test generalization.
- Templates: “<E_i> ->R1”, next token expected is <E_j>. For reverse evaluation, prompt “<E_j> <-R1”. For two-hop: “<E_i> >>R1”. Add distractor text for fluency measurement.

Models
- Ours: GPT-2 small + AdapterEntityRelHead (k=32, α_min=0.35, K=200).
- Baselines:
  1) Base CE fine-tune forward-only (no adapter; small LR on lm_head rows for entities via row-localization U P^T).
  2) Non-invertible adapter: same head but no orthogonality/composition constraints (plain linear map), no symmetric-KL.
  3) Data augmentation upper bound: forward + reverse labels (not always available in practice; acts as oracle).
  4) LRE post-hoc: After base CE finetune, fit a Procrustes map z_A -> E_B on training pairs; apply at inference; no training-time constraints.

Training
- Use forward CE on train pairs only; include composition loss on batches with (A->B, B->C) to predict C from A via composed core; include invertibility and symmetric-KL. Freeze transformer; unfreeze lm_head last 10% epochs with LR 5e-6.

Metrics and protocol
- Accuracy@1 on forward, reverse, two-hop. Report reverse gap and comp gap (forward vs two-hop).
- Cycle error: Frobenius norm of M_r^T M_r−I, and ||M_{r^{-1}}M_r−I||.
- Symmetric-KL residual between adapter outputs for A-> and swapped B<-.
- Perplexity on 100k tokens of WikiText-2 validation (no relation prompts) to assess fluency drift.
- Gate usage: fraction of entity positions with α>0.1; average α.
- Throughput overhead vs base (tokens/sec, wall-clock per batch).
- Ablations: k∈{16,32,64}, α_min∈{0.2,0.35,0.5}, K∈{50,200,1000}, with/without R_r, with/without symmetric-KL, Householder vs matrix_exp.

Expected outcomes
- Ours: Reverse and two-hop accuracies near forward (small gaps), low cycle error, near-base perplexity, modest overhead ≤20%.
- Baseline CE: High forward, low reverse/two-hop; large reverse gap.
- Non-invertible: Improves reverse but worse two-hop and higher cycle error; more drift in perplexity.
- Data-aug: Strong reverse but requires reverse labels; two-hop not guaranteed; perplexity may drift more without gating.
- LRE post-hoc: Moderate reverse improvements, weaker two-hop, less stable under decoding.

Minimal Python sketch (dataset + train loop shells)
- Synthetic pairs
  def make_bijection(n):
      idx = torch.randperm(n).tolist()
      return {i: idx[i] for i in range(n)}
  def synth_dataset(entities, pi, split=(0.6,0.2,0.2)):
      # returns train forward pairs, dev/test reverse and chain triples

- Training step
  def train_step(batch):
      # batch contains tokenized prompts, target ids, relation_id, entity_spans
      with torch.no_grad():
          out = base_model(input_ids, attention_mask, output_hidden_states=True)
      logits_base = out.logits[:, -1, :]
      h_t = out.hidden_states[-1][:, -1, :]
      cand_ids = topk_ids(logits_base, K)
      l_adapter, alpha = adapter(h_t, rel_id=batch['rel_id'], candidate_ids=cand_ids)
      l_mix = mix_logits(logits_base.index_select(1, cand_ids), l_adapter, alpha)
      loss_ce = F.cross_entropy(l_mix, map_to_topk_targets(batch['target_ids'], cand_ids))
      loss = loss_ce + lambda_align*align_loss(...) + lambda_inv*inv_pen(...) + ...
      loss.backward(); opt.step(); opt.zero_grad()

- Eval computes accuracy by comparing top-1 candidate to ground-truth.


Experiment 2: Natural Relations (Wikidata kinship, morphology, bilingual lexicon)
Goal
- Demonstrate gains on real relations without reverse labels, preserving grammar.

Data
- Kinship (Wikidata): Extract triplets (parent_of, child_of) for persons with aliases mapping to tokenizer tokens; ensure tokenization produces single tokens for names via added tokens (or byte-level BPE spans considered entities). Train only on parent_of (A parent_of B). Evaluate child_of (B child_of A) and two-hop grandparent (A→B, B→C ⇒ A→→C). Size: ~10k pairs.
- Morphology:
  - English singular↔plural noun pairs (UniMorph or wordfreq + inflect library). Train singular->plural only; eval plural->singular.
  - English present->past tense; eval reverse.
  - Compose tense transformations: present->past->past_participle and check A→→C.
  Prompt format: “singular: dog -> plural:” expecting “dogs”; “past_of: go ->” expecting “went”.
- Bilingual lexicon (en↔es): Small 5k dictionary (MUSE or wiktionary scrape). Train en->es only; eval es->en. Use simple templates: “en2es: dog ->”, reverse prompt: “es2en: perro ->”.

Modeling
- Same adapter head with relation IDs {parent_of, child_of, grandparent (composed), sing2plur, plur2sing (tied inverse), en2es, es2en (tied inverse), ...}. Define inverse ties via M_{r^{-1}}=M_r^T; compositions constructed on the fly for kinship two-hop.
- Multi-token handling: If entity spans tokenize into multiple sub-tokens, pool hidden states over span (mean) for z and score the first sub-token; optionally copy-attend for subsequent sub-tokens (simple heuristic: follow base logits for continuation once the first token matches target candidate’s first token).

Training
- Forward CE only for forward directions: parent_of, sing2plur, present2past, en2es. Include composition loss using kinship two-hop chains constructed from (A parent_of B, B parent_of C) pairs. Include symmetry, orthogonality, trust region losses. Freeze base model. Gate schedule as in Experiment 1.

Evaluation
- Accuracy@1 on reverse directions: child_of, plur2sing, past2present, es2en.
- Two-hop kinship accuracy (grandparent_of) and consistency with composed scorer M_{parent}^2.
- BLEU-1/2 on morphological reverse tasks (for multi-token forms) and bilingual mapping outputs.
- Perplexity on WikiText-2 validation to assess fluency drift.
- Gate activation rate; qualitative examples; error typology.
- Compare with baselines: base CE (forward-only), non-invertible adapter, data-aug upper bound (forward+reverse), LRE post-hoc.

Expected outcomes
- REA-Cycle++ significantly reduces reversal gap and achieves strong two-hop kinship accuracy without reverse labels, while maintaining near-base perplexity.

Python notes (preprocessing snippets)
- Kinship extraction
  # Assume wikidata triples (Qids) mapped to surface forms via aliases; filter by tokenizer coverage
  pairs = [(nameA, nameB) for (A,B) in wikidata_parent_of if cover(A) and cover(B)]
  train = forward_parent_only(pairs)
  chains = build_chains(pairs)  # (A,B,C)
- Morph

  p = inflect.engine()
  sing2plur = [(w, p.plural(w)) for w in vocab if p.singular_noun(w) == False and p.plural(w)]
  present2past = load_unimorph_pairs(...)
- Bilingual

Experiment 3: Interpretability, Constraints, and LRE Distillation Ablation
Goal
- Validate the necessity of group/invertibility constraints and show geometry is auditable. Assess the benefit of LRE/Jacobian distillation.

Protocol
- Train three variants on Experiment 1 (and optionally Experiment 2 datasets):
  A) Full REA-Cycle++ (orthogonal core + composition + symmetric-KL).
  B) No group constraints (plain linear maps, no orthogonality penalties, no composition loss).
  C) Full + LRE distillation from a teacher (few-shot LRE from the frozen base or an ICL-prompted teacher).

Analyses
- Spectral diagnostics:
  - Eigenvalues of M_r lie on unit circle (angle histogram), ||M_r^T M_r − I||_F.
  - Residual norm ||R_r||_2 over epochs (if enabled).
- Subspace quality:
  - Explained variance of P on entity states; isotropy of z (covariance ≈ I).
  - Alignment between Ĥ_r z_A and E_B (cosine similarity), compared across A/B/C.
- LRE distillation:
  - Construct teacher Jacobian J_r by least-squares: argmin_J ||J Z_A − E_B||_F on a 512-pair subset; project to skew space S=(J−J^T)/2; minimize ||log M_r − S||_F.
  - Compare reverse and two-hop accuracies with/without distillation at equal compute.
- Fluency and gate usage: compare perplexity and α distributions across A/B/C.

Expected outcomes
- Variant A shows low cycle error and strong reverse/two-hop without fluency drift.
- Variant B improves reverse less, fails at two-hop, higher cycle error, more drift.
- Variant C matches or exceeds A in low-resource regimes; faster convergence; cleaner spectra.

Python metrics sketches
- Spectral checks
  def cycle_error(M):
      return torch.linalg.norm(M.T @ M - torch.eye(M.size(0), device=M.device))
  def eig_angles(M):
      w = torch.linalg.eigvals(M)
      return torch.angle(w)  # expecting magnitudes ~1
- LRE teacher
  def lre_teacher(Z, Y):  # Z: [n,k] z_A; Y: [n,k] E_B
      J, *_ = torch.linalg.lstsq(Z, Y)
      S = 0.5 * (J - J.T)
      return S
- Distill loss term
  loss_lre = F.mse_loss(matrix_log(M_r), S_teacher)


Reproducibility and compute
- Hardware: Single A100 or 3090; each experiment ≤2 hours wall-clock with GPT-2 small and k≤64.
- Seeds: {0,1,2}; report mean±std.
- Logging: wandb or TensorBoard; log metrics above and adapter toggle rate.

Success criteria across experiments
- Reverse parity: Reverse accuracy within 5–10% of forward without using reverse labels.
- Two-hop competence: Two-hop accuracy significantly above baselines and close to composed scorer predictions.
- Fluency preservation: Perplexity drift ≤3% from frozen base; adapter can be toggled off to recover base behavior.
- Interpretability: Low cycle error, spectra on unit circle, stable τ_r and α usage; clear improvements from constraints and LRE distillation.
experimental_design.experiment_details:
REA-Cycle++: End-to-end experimental plan, integrated protocols, and PyTorch/HF implementation sketches

1) Common setup (applies to all experiments)
- Models
  - Base: HuggingFace GPT-2 small (124M) by default; optionally Pythia-70M (EleutherAI/pythia-70m-deduped) for speed. Use HF Transformers >=4.41, PyTorch >=2.2, CUDA.
  - Tokenizer: Augment with relation markers and synthetic entity tokens when needed. Example added tokens: ["->R1", "<-R1", ">>R1", "->R2", "<-R2", ">>R2"].
